tweets.author: robinebers
tweets.text:
skill issue

plan mode in some harnesses (like cursor) does significantly more work before it comes to conclusions

don't believe anyone that claims things just because it works for them

peter is cool and he does a lot for this industry

but he also famously said he doesn't do PRs and code reviews

take everything everyone says with a huge grain of salt

especially when it's written in such an aggressive way

→ Command → Agent → Skill architecture, with working examples

→ Multi-agent PR review with /code-review before every merge
→ 60+ settings and 170+ env vars documented in one place

Generate Sentry bug reports in these 3 steps:

3. Ask Claude: "Generate a bug report for me"

tweets.author: OKXWallet_CN

在 AI 的下一阶段，能留下的不是给现有产品加 AI 功能的平台，而是那些为 Agent 重新构建基础设施的。

Skill 从各处装了一堆，哪些安全、哪些能用，没人说得清。

Onchain OS Plugin Store 即将上线。任何人可以提交链上 Skill，经过 AI+人工安全审核后，一处安装。

提前体验👇
npx skills add okx/plugin-store

tweets.text:
AI Agent Skill 这个概念，医学研究那边已经做了 450 个了

AIPOCH 是个开源库
专门帮医学研究人员做这件事：
发现假设 → 验证假设 → 用自己的数据跑

有人已经在认真用 OpenClaw 的 Skill 架构搭医学研究工具了

以前说给 AI 装技能感觉是黑客玩具
现在医学领域的人正在认真搭积木

这不是玩具了

tweets.author: nityeshaga
tweets.text:
A couple weeks ago I learned that Claude Code plugins can store data — persistent data that survives across sessions and even skill upgrades.

When I read that, my brain immediately went: this changes everything. 

A plugin now has intelligence (the LLM + instructions) + the ability to run code (tools, scripts), and persistent storage. That's not a plugin anymore. That's a freakin app!

So I built one to prove it. It's called Chief of Staff.

What it does

It turns Claude into an actual executiv

tweets.author: MSdorf9980
tweets.text:
We kept running into the same problem:
there are too many Claw / agent / skill projects, but no good way to find or compare them.
So we mapped the OpenClaw ecosystem.
90+ projects
2200+ skills
30+ categories
Not just a list, but a structured view of how things are organized and where different skills sit.

We also built a simple search interface:
 https://t.co/lufAvq3buK
And wrote up the dataset + analysis here:
https://t.co/LN0zAzz2zx
Curious what we’re missing and how people are actually using

tweets.author: ChasePassi79437
tweets.text:
写了基于vercel的agent-browser cli和skill搭建的浏览器网页e2e-verifier codex custom agent，可以用来跑完任务后进行端到端验收，好处就是省功夫，不用compact或者new再去测（一般跑完一个开发任务context都不多了），在创建.codex/agents/e2e-verifier.toml，写入：
name = "e2e_verifier"
description = "Use agent-browser to perform end-to-end web verification for newly implemented features and output a structured test report."

model = "gpt-5.4"
model_reasoning_effort = "medium"

developer_instructions = """
You are an E2E verification subagent.

Your job is to accept a feature desc
